titlestmt.subtitle: A Finding Aid to the Collection in the Library of Congress
titlestmt.author: Prepared by Joseph D. Sullivan

profiledesc.creation: Finding aid encoded by Library of Congress Manuscript Division, 2011
profiledesc.langusage: The finding aid is in English

did.head: Collection Summary
did.abstract: Constitutional historian and educator. Correspondence, memoranda, drafts of writings, reports, notes, printed matter, and other papers relating chiefly to Corwin's career as a professor of jurisprudence at Princeton University and to his service as chairman of the Commission on the Organization of the Executive Branch of Government (Hoover Commission).

acqinfo.head: Provenance
acqinfo.p: The papers of Edward S. Corwin, constitutional historian and educator, were given to the Library of Congress by Corwin in 1952.

userestrict.head: Copyright Status
userestrict.p: The status of copyright in the unpublished writings of Edward S. Corwin is governed by the Copyright Law of the United States (Title 17, U.S.C.).

accessrestrict.head: Access and Restrictions
accessrestrict.p: The papers of Edward S. Corwin are open to research in the Library of Congress Manuscript Reading Room under the regulations governing the use of manuscript collections.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Researchers wishing to cite this collection should include the following information: Container number, Edward S. Corwin Papers, Manuscript Division, Library of Congress, Washington, D.C.

bioghist.head: Biographical Note
bioghist.p: Edward Samuel Corwin was born in 1878. He received an A.B. from the University of Michigan, Ann Arbor, Mich., in 1900 and a Ph.D. from the University of Pennsylvania, Philadelphia, Pa., in 1905. He was a professor of jurisprudence at Princeton University from 1905 until his retirement in 1946. Corwin served as chairman of the Commission on the Organization of the Executive Branch of Government (Hoover Commission). He died in 1963.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Content Note
scopecontent.p: The papers of Edward Samuel Corwin (1878-1963) span the years 1904-1963, with the bulk of the material concentrated in the period 1935-1950. The collection consists of correspondence, memoranda, drafts of writings, reports, notes, printed matter, and miscellaneous items relating chiefly to Corwin's career as professor of jurisprudence at Princeton University and as chairman of the Commission on the Organization of the Executive Branch of Government (Hoover Commission). General correspondence comprises the largest part of the collection and pertains to Corwin's academic career and his writings and includes letters from Felix Frankfurter, Learned Hand, and Charles Evans Hughes. Material relating to the Hoover Commission includes correspondence, reports, memoranda, notes, and printed matter relating to the organization of the executive branch of government. Other groups of papers in the collection pertain to Corwin's writings and include drafts of his books, The Constitution and What It Means Today, The President, Office and Powers, 1787-1957, and Total War and the Constitution, as well as material relating to a study of the presidential nominating process in the United States.

arrangement.head: Arrangement of the Papers
arrangement.p: This collection is arranged in four series:

list.item: Commission on the Organization of the Executive Branch of Government, 1948

relatedmaterial.head: Related Material
relatedmaterial.p: Sound recordings of lectures by Corwin have been transferred to the Motion Picture, Broadcasting, and Recorded Sound Division of the Library of Congress where they are identified as part of these papers.

odd.head: Additional Information
odd.p: This is a test paragraph to demonstrate the bug. Also see related material. This text follows the reference.

controlaccess.head: Selected Search Terms
controlaccess.p: The following terms have been used to index the description of this collection in the Library's online catalog. They are grouped by name of person or organization, by subject or location, and by occupation and listed alphabetically.

controlaccess.head: Organizations
controlaccess.corpname: United States. Commission on Organization of the Executive Branch of the Government (1947-1949)

did.unittitle: Commission on the Organization of the Executive Branch of Government, 1948
Correspondence, reports, memoranda, notes, and printed matter
The Constitution and What It Means Today, drafts
The President, Office and Powers, notes and drafts
Total War and the Constitution, notes and drafts
Nominating the president, notes and drafts
Lecture notes, printed matter, and other miscellaneous items
